_comment: Optional per-issue enrichment for create-issues.mjs. PLANNING.md stays the source of truth for inventory/order/labels/deps; this only adds goal + scope bullets. Add entries here to make any issue's body richer; missing entries fall back to sensible template defaults. See PLANNING.md ‚Üí 'CI/CD pipeline' for the full pipeline design that T-00/T-01/T-05‚ÄìT-09 implement.

T-00.goal: Stand up Stage 1 of the pipeline: the code-quality CI workflow. The repo has no `.github/workflows/` yet, so this is the first gate every later PR depends on.
scope: Runner: all jobs `runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64]` (the `cutline-runner-1` self-hosted runner) ‚Äî not GitHub-hosted. Don't assume a clean VM; gate fork PRs so untrusted code never runs on the self-hosted runner.
Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml` triggered on `pull_request` and `push` to `master`.
Job hardening: `permissions: { contents: read }`; `concurrency` group per-ref with `cancel-in-progress: true`; `timeout-minutes`; pin every Action to a full commit SHA.
Setup: checkout ‚Üí `oven-sh/setup-bun` ‚Üí cache Bun store + Playwright browsers ‚Üí `bun install --frozen-lockfile`.

Quality steps (fail fast, each its own step): `prettier --check .` ‚Üí `eslint .` ‚Üí `bun run check` (svelte-check + tsc, zero errors) ‚Üí `bun run test:unit -- --run` (Vitest client browser + server projects) ‚Üí `playwright install --with-deps chromium` + `bun run test:e2e`.
Do NOT add coverage (T-01), security (T-05‚ÄìT-07), or build/SBOM (T-08) here ‚Äî those are separate workflows.
Acceptance: a red PR (lint/type/test failure) blocks; a clean PR passes; job names are stable so T-09 can require them.

T-01.goal: Stage 2: enforce coverage thresholds in CI. No coverage provider is installed yet.
scope: Runner: the coverage job runs on the `cutline-runner-1` self-hosted runner ‚Äî `runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64]`.
`bun add -D @vitest/coverage-v8`.

Add a `test.coverage` block to `vite.config.ts`: provider `v8`, reporters `['text','html','lcov']`, and per-area `thresholds` from PLANNING.md ‚Üí Testing strategy (utils ‚â•95, editor ‚â•90, ui ‚â•85, components ‚â•80, server ‚â•85, overall ‚â•80).
Enable coverage for BOTH the `client` (browser) and `server` projects; verify thresholds aggregate across both.
Wire `bun run test:unit -- --run --coverage` into `ci.yml`; upload the lcov/html report as an artifact.
PR fails when coverage drops below threshold. Pre-T-01 PRs mark the coverage checkbox `N/A (pre-T-01)`.

T-05.goal: Stage 3 (safe code): detect secrets/credentials before they reach history.
scope: Runner: `runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64]` (the `cutline-runner-1` self-hosted runner) ‚Äî not GitHub-hosted.
Add `gitleaks` to `.github/workflows/security.yml` on `pull_request` + `push`; add a scheduled (weekly) full-history scan.
Commit `.gitleaks.toml` with the ruleset and a documented allowlist for known false positives.
Enable repo **secret scanning** + **push protection** (Settings ‚Üí Code security & analysis) and note it in the issue.
Least-privilege `permissions`; SHA-pinned action; fail the PR on any detected secret.
Acceptance: a test commit containing a fake token is blocked by the workflow.
Runner: `runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64]` (the `cutline-runner-1` self-hosted runner) ‚Äî not GitHub-hosted.
Add a dependency-audit job to `.github/workflows/security.yml`: `bun audit` failing on high/critical (augment with `google/osv-scanner` for transitive coverage).
Add `actions/dependency-review-action` on `pull_request` to block known-vulnerable deps and disallowed licenses (define an allowed-license policy).
Add `.github/dependabot.yml` with weekly updates for the npm/bun ecosystem AND `github-actions`.
Supply-chain hardening: pin ALL third-party Actions to commit SHAs (audit with `zizmor`); ensure `bun.lock` is committed and every job uses `--frozen-lockfile`.
Acceptance: a PR adding a known-vulnerable dependency is blocked; Dependabot opens update PRs.

T-07.goal: Stage 5 (safe code): static application security testing via CodeQL.
scope: Runner: `runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64]` (the `cutline-runner-1` self-hosted runner) ‚Äî not GitHub-hosted. Ensure the runner meets CodeQL's resource needs.
Add `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` using `github/codeql-action` (init/analyze) for language `javascript-typescript`.
Use the `security-extended` query suite; run on `pull_request`, `push: master`, and a weekly schedule.
`permissions: { security-events: write, contents: read, actions: read }`; SHA-pinned actions.
Results surface in the repo **Security** tab; PR fails on new high-severity alerts.
Acceptance: CodeQL completes and uploads results; the Security tab shows the scan.

T-08.goal: Stage 6 (safe binaries): verify the build, produce an SBOM, and scan the output.
scope: Runner: `runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64]` (the `cutline-runner-1` self-hosted runner) ‚Äî not GitHub-hosted.
Add `.github/workflows/build.yml`: `bun install --frozen-lockfile` ‚Üí `bun run build` must succeed; upload the build output as a retention-limited artifact.
Generate a CycloneDX **SBOM** (`@cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm` or `anchore/sbom-action`) and attach it to the run.
Run **Trivy** (filesystem + config/misconfig scan) and fail on high/critical.
Container/release path (guarded ‚Äî only when a `Dockerfile`/release artifact exists): build image ‚Üí Trivy image scan ‚Üí `actions/attest-build-provenance` ‚Üí verify before any deploy.
Note in the issue: the app is currently a SvelteKit web build (no native binary); this stage hardens the build output and is ready for containerized deploys.
Acceptance: build artifact + SBOM are attached to the run; Trivy gate is enforced.

T-09.goal: Stage 7: turn the workflows into enforced merge requirements and route reviews.
scope: Finalize `.github/pull_request_template.md` (quality + security checklist ‚Äî already scaffolded).
Add `.github/CODEOWNERS` routing reviews (e.g. `* @gabepsilva`; refine per area later).

Configure **branch protection** on `master` (repo Settings or `gh api`): require a PR, ‚â•1 approving review, dismiss stale approvals, require branches up to date, linear history, no force-push, and require the status checks from T-00/T-01/T-05/T-06/T-07/T-08 to pass.
Document the exact required-check job names in the issue (taken from the merged workflows).
Acceptance: a PR cannot merge while any required check is red or without an approving review.

T-10.goal: Stage 8a: package Cutline as a production container image ready for Kubernetes.
scope: Replace `@sveltejs/adapter-auto` with `@sveltejs/adapter-node` (add `svelte.config.js` if needed). Listen on `0.0.0.0:${PORT:-3000}`.
Add multi-stage `Dockerfile` at repo root: `oven/bun` deps ‚Üí build ‚Üí slim runtime (`oven/bun` or `node:22-alpine`). Pin base-image digests like `jpegs3/cloud-jpegs3-frontend/Dockerfile`.
Add `.dockerignore` (exclude `.git`, `node_modules`, `.svelte-kit`, test artifacts, `*.db`).
Add `GET /healthz` (`src/routes/healthz/+server.ts`) returning `200` + `{ ok: true }` for K8s probes (pattern: `jpegs3-website` `/healthz`).

Runtime env contract (document in `infra/k8s/README.md`): `ORIGIN=https://cutline.i.psilva.org`, `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` (32+ chars), `DATABASE_URL=file:/tmp/cutline.db` (ephemeral SQLite ‚Äî persistent DB deferred). **Do not wire GitHub OAuth yet.**
Enable the guarded Trivy **image** scan step in `.github/workflows/build.yml` (`docker build` ‚Üí `cutline:ci` ‚Üí scan).
Reference: `jpegs3/cloud-jpegs3-frontend/Dockerfile` (bun multi-stage); SvelteKit adapter-node docs.
Acceptance: `docker build -t cutline:local .` succeeds; container serves app + `/healthz`; Trivy image scan runs in CI.

T-11.goal: Stage 8b: Kubernetes manifests for namespace `cutline` with ingress to `cutline.i.psilva.org`.
scope: Add `infra/k8s/` using kustomize (`base/` + `overlays/prod/`). Resources: Namespace, Deployment, Service (ClusterIP :80 ‚Üí app :3000), Ingress, cert-manager `Certificate`, ConfigMap.

Ingress host: **`cutline.i.psilva.org`** ‚Äî `ingressClassName: nginx`; annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-dns01`, `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: "true"` (match `homelab7/plex/k8s/ingress.yaml` + `image-works` HSTS annotations).

Deployment hardening (match `jpegs3/website/infra/manifests/overlays/prod/deployment.yaml`): `runAsNonRoot`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem`, dropped caps, http readiness/liveness on `/healthz`, resource requests/limits, `emptyDir` for `/tmp` + SQLite path.

ConfigMap: `ORIGIN`, `PORT`, `DATABASE_URL=file:/tmp/cutline.db`. Secret `cutline-app` for `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` ‚Äî document one-time bootstrap: `kubectl -n cutline create secret generic cutline-app --from-literal=BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=‚Ä¶`.
If GHCR is private: `imagePullSecrets` (`cutline-regcred`) ‚Äî document creation from a PAT.
Placeholder image tag in Deployment (CD updates it): `ghcr.io/gabepsilva/cutline:bootstrap`.
Add `infra/k8s/README.md` with one-time bootstrap: `kubectl apply -k infra/k8s/overlays/prod`.
**Out of scope:** persistent DB volume, CNPG/Postgres, GitHub OAuth secrets, Argo CD Application.
Acceptance: `kubectl apply -k infra/k8s/overlays/prod --dry-run=server` passes; Certificate + Ingress describe `cutline.i.psilva.org`; docs cover secret bootstrap.

T-12.goal: Stage 8c: CD workflow ‚Äî build image on merge, push to GHCR, roll out to `cutline` namespace, verify route.
scope: Runner: `runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64]` (`cutline-runner-1`) ‚Äî same host has `kubectl` access to `tk8s` (admin context). Trusted refs only.
Add `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` triggered on `push: master` (paths: app source, `Dockerfile`, `infra/k8s/**`, workflow) + `workflow_dispatch`.
`permissions: { contents: read, packages: write }`; `concurrency` per ref; SHA-pinned Actions.

Steps (pattern: `jpegs3/.github/workflows/build-WEBSITE.yml`): checkout ‚Üí `docker/login-action` to `ghcr.io` ‚Üí `docker/build-push-action` with buildx cache ‚Üí tag `ghcr.io/gabepsilva/cutline:YYYY-MM-DD_${GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER}` + `:latest`.
Rollout: `kubectl -n cutline set image deployment/cutline cutline=$DATE_TAG` ‚Üí `kubectl rollout status` (300s timeout).
Post-deploy verify: resolve ingress LB IP, `curl -fsS --resolve cutline.i.psilva.org:443:$IP https://cutline.i.psilva.org/healthz`.
Do **not** auto-deploy on PRs; production rollout is `master` only. Optional manual `workflow_dispatch` input for tag promotion later.
Depends on T-10 (image builds) and T-11 (manifests applied once on cluster).
Acceptance: merge to `master` builds + pushes image and updates the running Deployment; health check passes via ingress.

M1-01.goal: Extract the design's global tokens into `src/lib/styles/tokens.css` as the canonical, themeable source consumed by every component. This is a hard gate: M1-04+ / M2 / M4‚ÄìM6 must not start until token names are final and merged.
scope: Define CSS custom properties for the full Design tokens reference in PLANNING.md: surfaces (`--surface-base`‚Ä¶`--surface-active`), borders (`--border-faint`‚Ä¶`--border-7`), text (`--text-bright`‚Ä¶`--text-placeholder`), accent/danger/highlight, typography (`--font-sans`, `--font-mono`), radii, z-index, motion, elevation, scrollbar, and fixed layout dims (`--sidebar-w`, `--topbar-h`, `--rail-w`, `--timeline-h`, `--track-label-w`, `--ruler-h`).
Default `--accent: #ff6a3d`; derive accent/danger tints from the accent variable via `color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) N%, transparent)` so the 4 design accents work without hardcoding orange tints.
Expose tokens on `:root`; do not include component markup or global resets (those are M1-02).
Publish the final token-name list in this issue/PR description before dependent issues start (consumers reference these exact names ‚Äî never raw hex).
